sections.label: Section 138
sections.original: σύγγνωθί μοι · τηλοῦ γὰρ οἰκῶ τῶν ἀγρῶν .
sections.translation: Pardon me ; for I dwell afar in the country . But tell me the thing which has been made to miscarry .

sections.label: Section 140
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐ θέμις πλὴν τοῖς μαθηταῖσιν λέγειν .
sections.translation: It is not lawful to mention it , except to disciples .

sections.label: Section 141
sections.original: λέγε νυν ἐμοὶ θαρρῶν · ἐγὼ γὰρ οὑτοσὶ
sections.translation: Tell it , then , to me without fear ; for I here am come as a disciple to the thinking-shop .

sections.label: Section 143
sections.original: λέξω . νομίσαι δὲ ταῦτα χρὴ μυστήρια .
sections.translation: I will tell you ; but you must regard these as mysteries . Socrates lately asked Chaerephon about a flea , how many of its own feet it jumped ; for after having bit the eyebrow of Chaerephon , it leaped away onto the head of Socrates .

sections.label: Section 148
sections.original: πῶς δῆτα διεμέτρησε ;
sections.translation: How then did he measure this ?

sections.label: Section 148b
sections.original: δεξιώτατα .
sections.translation: Most cleverly . He melted some wax ; and then took the flea

sections.label: Section 150
sections.original: ἐνέβαψεν ἐς τὸν κηρὸν αὐτῆς τὼ πόδε ,
sections.translation: and dipped its feet in the wax ; and then a pair of Persian slippers stuck to it when cooled . Having gently loosened these , he measured back the distance .

sections.label: Section 153
sections.original: ὦ Ζεῦ βασιλεῦ τῆς λεπτότητος τῶν φρενῶν .
sections.translation: O King Jupiter ! What subtlety of thought !

sections.label: Section 154
sections.original: τί δῆτʼ ἂν ἕτερον εἰ πύθοιο Σωκράτους
sections.translation: What then would you say if you heard another contrivance of Socrates ?

sections.label: Section 155b
sections.original: ποῖον ; ἀντιβολῶ κάτειπέ μοι .
sections.translation: Of what kind ? Tell me , I beseech you !

sections.label: Section 156
sections.original: ἀνήρετʼ αὐτὸν Χαιρεφῶν ὁ Σφήττιος
sections.translation: Chaerephon the Sphettian asked him whether he thought gnats buzzed through the mouth or the breech .

sections.label: Section 159
sections.original: τί δῆτʼ ἐκεῖνος εἶπε περὶ τῆς ἐμπίδος ;
sections.translation: What , then , did he say about the gnat ?

sections.label: Section 160
sections.original: ἔφασκεν εἶναι τοὔντερον τῆς ἐμπίδος
sections.translation: He said the intestine of the gnat was narrow and that the wind went forcibly through it , being slender , straight to the breech ; and then that the rump , being hollow where it is adjacent to the narrow part , resounded through the violence of the wind .

sections.label: Section 165
sections.original: σάλπιγξ ὁ πρωκτός ἐστιν ἄρα τῶν ἐμπίδων .
sections.translation: The rump of the gnats then is a trumpet ! Oh , thrice happy he for his sharp-sightedness ! Surely a defendant might easily get acquitted who understands the intestine of the gnat .

sections.label: Section 169
sections.original: πρώην δέ γε γνώμην μεγάλην ἀφῃρέθη
sections.translation: But he was lately deprived of a great idea by a lizard .

sections.label: Section 170b
sections.original: τίνα τρόπον ; κάτειπέ μοι .
sections.translation: In what way ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 171
sections.original: ζητοῦντος αὐτοῦ τῆς σελήνης τὰς ὁδοὺς
sections.translation: As he was investigating the courses of the moon and her revolutions , then as he was gaping upward a lizard in the darkness dropped upon him from the roof .

sections.label: Section 174
sections.original: ἥσθην γαλεώτῃ καταχέσαντι Σωκράτους .
sections.translation: I am amused at a lizard ’ s having dropped on Socrates .

sections.label: Section 175
sections.original: ἐχθὲς δέ γʼ ἡμῖν δεῖπνον οὐκ ἦν ἑσπέρας .
sections.translation: Yesterday evening there was no supper for us .

sections.label: Section 176
sections.original: εἶεν · τί οὖν πρὸς τἄλφιτʼ ἐπαλαμήσατο ;
sections.translation: Well . What then did he contrive for provisions ?

sections.label: Section 177
sections.original: κατὰ τῆς τραπέζης καταπάσας λεπτὴν τέφραν
sections.translation: He sprinkled fine ashes on the table , and bent a little spit , and then took it as a pair of compasses and filched a cloak from the Palaestra .

sections.label: Section 180
sections.original: τί δῆτʼ ἐκεῖνον τὸν Θαλῆν θαυμάζομεν ;
sections.translation: Why then do we admire Thales ? Open open quickly the thinking-shop , and show to me Socrates as quickly as possible . For I desire to be a disciple . Come , open the door . The door of the thinking-shop opens and the pupils of Socrates are seen all with their heads fixed on the ground , while Socrates himself is seen suspended in the air in a basket . O Hercules , from what country are these wild beasts ?

sections.label: Section 185
sections.original: τί ἐθαύμασας ; τῷ σοι δοκοῦσιν εἰκέναι ;
sections.translation: What do you wonder at ? To what do they seem to you to be like ?

sections.label: Section 186
sections.original: τοῖς ἐκ Πύλου ληφθεῖσι τοῖς Λακωνικοῖς .
sections.translation: To the Spartans who were taken at Pylos . But why in the world do these look upon the ground ?

sections.label: Section 188
sections.original: ζητοῦσιν οὗτοι τὰ κατὰ γῆς .
sections.translation: They are in search of the things below the earth .

sections.label: Section 188b
sections.original: βολβοὺς ἄρα
sections.translation: Then they are searching for roots . Do not , then , trouble yourselves about this ;

sections.label: Section 190
sections.original: ἐγὼ γὰρ οἶδʼ ἵνʼ εἰσὶ μεγάλοι καὶ καλοί .
sections.translation: for I know where there are large and fine ones . Why , what are these doing , who are bent down so much ?

sections.label: Section 192
sections.original: οὗτοι δʼ ἐρεβοδιφῶσιν ὑπὸ τὸν Τάρταρον .
sections.translation: These are groping about in darkness under Tartarus .

sections.label: Section 193
sections.original: τί δῆθʼ ὁ πρωκτὸς ἐς τὸν οὐρανὸν βλέπει ;
sections.translation: Why then does their rump look toward heaven ?

sections.label: Section 194
sections.original: αὐτὸς καθʼ αὑτὸν ἀστρονομεῖν διδάσκεται .
sections.translation: It is getting taught astronomy alone by itself . Turning to the pupils . But go in , lest he meet with us .

sections.label: Section 196
sections.original: μήπω γε μήπω γʼ · ἀλλʼ ἐπιμεινάντων , ἵνα
sections.translation: Not yet , not yet ; but let them remain , that I may communicate to them a little matter of my own .

sections.label: Section 198
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐχ οἷόν τʼ αὐτοῖσι πρὸς τὸν ἀέρα
sections.translation: It is not permitted to them to remain without in the open air for a very long time .

sections.label: Section 200
sections.original: πρὸς τῶν θεῶν τί γὰρ τάδʼ ἐστίν ; εἰπέ μοι .
sections.translation: ( discovering a variety of mathematical instruments ). Why , what is this , in the name of heaven ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 201
sections.original: ἀστρονομία μὲν αὑτηί .
sections.translation: This is Astronomy .

sections.label: Section 201b
sections.original: τουτὶ δὲ τί ;
sections.translation: But what is this ?

sections.label: Section 202
sections.original: γεωμετρία .
sections.translation: Geometry .

sections.label: Section 202b
sections.original: τοῦτʼ οὖν τί ἐστι χρήσιμον ;
sections.translation: What then is the use of this ?

sections.label: Section 203
sections.original: γῆν ἀναμετρῆσαι .
sections.translation: To measure out the land .

sections.label: Section 203b
sections.original: πότερα τὴν κληρουχικήν ;
sections.translation: What belongs to an allotment ?

sections.label: Section 204
sections.original: οὔκ , ἀλλὰ τὴν σύμπασαν .
sections.translation: No , but the whole earth .

sections.label: Section 204b
sections.original: ἀστεῖον λέγεις .
sections.translation: You tell me a clever notion ; for the contrivance is democratic and useful .

sections.label: Section 206
sections.original: αὕτη δέ σοι γῆς περίοδος πάσης . ὁρᾷς ;
sections.translation: See , here ’ s a map of the whole earth . Do you see ? This is Athens .

sections.label: Section 207b
sections.original: τί σὺ λέγεις ; οὐ πείθομαι ,
sections.translation: What say you ? I don ’ t believe you ; for I do not see the Dicasts sitting .

sections.label: Section 209
sections.original: ὡς τοῦτʼ ἀληθῶς Ἀττικὸν τὸ χωρίον .
sections.translation: Be assured that this is truly the Attic territory .

sections.label: Section 210
sections.original: καὶ ποῦ Κικυννῆς εἰσὶν οὑμοὶ δημόται ;
sections.translation: Why , where are my fellow-tribesmen of Cicynna ?

sections.label: Section 211
sections.original: ἐνταῦθʼ ἔνεισιν . ἡ δέ γʼ Εὔβοἰ , ὡς ὁρᾷς ,
sections.translation: Here they are . And Euboea here , as you see , is stretched out a long way by the side of it to a great distance .

sections.label: Section 213
sections.original: οἶδʼ · ὑπὸ γὰρ ἡμῶν παρετάθη καὶ Περικλέους .
sections.translation: I know that ; for it was stretched by us and Pericles . But where is Lacedaemon ?

sections.label: Section 214b
sections.original: ὅπου ʼστίν ; αὑτηί .
sections.translation: Where is it ? Here it is .

sections.label: Section 215
sections.original: ὡς ἐγγὺς ἡμῶν . τοῦτο πάνυ φροντίζετε ,
sections.translation: How near it is to us ! Pay great attention to this , to remove it very far from us .

sections.label: Section 217
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐχ οἷόν τε .
sections.translation: By Jupiter , it is not possible .

sections.label: Section 217b
sections.original: νὴ Δίʼ οἰμώξεσθʼ ἄρα .
sections.translation: Then you will weep for it . Looking up and discovering Socrates . Come , who is this man who is in the basket ?

sections.label: Section 219
sections.original: αὐτός .
sections.translation: Himself .

sections.label: Section 219b
sections.original: τίς αὐτός ;
sections.translation: Who ’ s Himself ?

sections.label: Section 219c
sections.original: Σωκράτης .
sections.translation: Socrates .

sections.label: Section 219d
sections.original: ὦ Σώκρατες .
sections.translation: O Socrates ! Come , you sir , call upon him loudly for me .

sections.label: Section 220a
sections.original: αὐτὸς μὲν οὖν σὺ κάλεσον · οὐ γάρ μοι σχολή .
sections.translation: Nay , rather , call him yourself ; for I have no leisure .

sections.label: Section 221
sections.original: ὦ Σώκρατες ,
sections.translation: Socrates ! My little Socrates !

sections.label: Section 223
sections.original: τί με καλεῖς ὦφήμερε ;
sections.translation: Why callest thou me , thou creature of a day ?

sections.label: Section 224
sections.original: πρῶτον μὲν ὅ τι δρᾷς ἀντιβολῶ κάτειπέ μοι .
sections.translation: First tell me , I beseech you , what are you doing .

sections.label: Section 225
sections.original: ἀεροβατῶ καὶ περιφρονῶ τὸν ἥλιον .
sections.translation: I am walking in the air , and speculating about the sun .
